text: As I move through these stark, polished spaces—gleaming glass walls and the clinical orderliness of a modern building—I feel an uncanny sense of dislocation. The sterile transparency of the first room unsettles me, as if there is nowhere to hide my thoughts. Upon entering the exhibition space, the bold "R+D" and the reflections about misunderstanding and longing strike a nerve. I feel exposed and watched in both body and intention, and wonder about the gap between what I mean and what others perceive. Here, thought and presence are laid bare, both stimulating and isolating.
